primary_meaning: to misunderstand, to be unaware of, to fail to recognize
secondary_meaning: to ignore or overlook the importance of something
usage_notes: Use with dangers, significance, risks; conjugate like connaitre
common_collocations: méconnaitre les risques, méconnaitre l'importance, méconnaitre la réalité
synonyms: ignorer, ne pas connaître, sous-estimer, oublier
image_context: Confused thinking and misunderstanding representing misapprehension
example_1: Ils ont méconnu les risques réels associés à ce projet ambitieux.
example_1_translation: They misunderstood the real risks associated with this ambitious project.
example_2: La population méconnait souvent les enjeux véritables de cette réforme complexe.
example_2_translation: The population is often unaware of the real issues of this complex reform.
example_3: Ne pas méconnaitre l'importance de cette découverte scientifique.
example_3_translation: Do not fail to recognize the importance of this scientific discovery.
pronunciation_tips: mé-ko-nè-tr. Three syllables with stress on first: MÉ-ko-nè-tr
memory_hook: Méconnaitre - me (not) + connaitre (to know) - not to know or understand
common_mistakes: Similar to 'connaitre' but with negative prefix; conjugates identically

primary_meaning: irreversible, unable to be reversed or undone
usage_notes: Use with damages, consequences, processes, decisions
common_collocations: dommages irréversibles, conséquences irréversibles, processus irréversible
image_context: Broken chain and irreversible damage representing permanent change
example_1: Les dommages environnementaux causés à l'écosystème sont profondément irréversibles.
example_1_translation: The environmental damage caused to the ecosystem is deeply irreversible.
example_2: Cette décision aura des conséquences irréversibles sur l'économie nationale.
example_2_translation: This decision will have irreversible consequences on the national economy.
example_3: Le changement climatique risque d'être irréversible sans action immédiate.
example_3_translation: Climate change risks becoming irreversible without immediate action.
pronunciation_tips: ir-ré-vér-sibl. Four syllables with stress on second: ir-RÉ-vér-sibl
memory_hook: Irréversible - ir (not) + réversible (reversible) - cannot be reversed
common_mistakes: Irreversible suggests permanence; irreplaceable suggests being unique

primary_meaning: cross-cutting, spanning multiple domains or disciplines
secondary_meaning: going across or cutting through transversely
common_collocations: compétences transversales, approche transversale, perspective transversale
image_context: Interconnected spheres and bridges representing interdisciplinary approach
example_1: Une approche transversale permet de mieux comprendre les phénomènes complexes.
example_1_translation: A cross-cutting approach allows for better understanding of complex phenomena.
example_2: Les compétences transversales sont de plus en plus valorisées sur le marché du travail.
example_2_translation: Cross-cutting skills are increasingly valued in the job market.
example_3: L'éducation transversale favorise la collaboration entre différentes disciplines.
example_3_translation: Transversal education encourages collaboration between different disciplines.
pronunciation_tips: tran-sver-sal. Three syllables with stress on third: tran-sver-SAL
memory_hook: Transversal - trans (across) + versal (turning) - cutting across domains
common_mistakes: Transversal vs vertical - transversal crosses many areas, vertical is narrow

primary_meaning: subordination, grammatical relationship of dependent to main clause
secondary_meaning: the state of being dependent or subject to another
usage_notes: Use in linguistics and grammar contexts with 'syntaxe', 'phrase'
common_collocations: subordination des phrases, analyse de la subordination, subordination syntaxique
example_1: La subordination des phrases permet d'exprimer des idées plus complexes et nuancées.
example_1_translation: The subordination of sentences allows expressing more complex and nuanced ideas.
example_2: L'analyse de la subordination est absolument essentielle en syntaxe française.
example_2_translation: The analysis of subordination is absolutely essential in French syntax.
example_3: Cette phrase montre une subordination multiples avec trois niveaux de dépendance.
example_3_translation: This sentence shows multiple subordination with three levels of dependency.
pronunciation_tips: sy-bor-di-na-sjON. Five syllables with nasal ending
memory_hook: Subordination - sub (under) + ordinare (to order) - placing under order
common_mistakes: Subordination is grammatical; not to be confused with social/political subordination

primary_meaning: to delegitimize, to undermine the legitimacy of
secondary_meaning: to cause to lose credibility or authority
usage_notes: Use with institutions, governments, authorities in political context
common_collocations: délégitimer le gouvernement, délégitimer l'autorité, délégitimer le pouvoir
image_context: Cracked foundation and damaged authority representing delegitimization
example_1: Les scandales récents ont fortement délégitimé le gouvernement aux yeux du public.
example_1_translation: Recent scandals have greatly delegitimized the government in public eyes.
example_2: Ces pratiques délégitiment progressivement les institutions démocratiques essentielles.
example_2_translation: These practices progressively delegitimize essential democratic institutions.
example_3: La corruption est un élément qui délégitame toute autorité politique.
example_3_translation: Corruption is an element that delegitimizes all political authority.
pronunciation_tips: dé-lé-ji-ti-mer. Five syllables with stress on first: DÉ-lé-ji-ti-mer
common_mistakes: Delegitimize refers to perceived legitimacy, not actual legality

primary_meaning: to rescind, to cancel or annul formally
secondary_meaning: to revoke a contract or agreement legally
common_collocations: rescinder un contrat, rescinder une clause, rescinder une décision
image_context: Legal documents being cancelled and torn representing rescission
example_1: Le tribunal a décidé de rescinder le contrat pour vice de forme flagrant.
example_1_translation: The court decided to rescind the contract due to a flagrant formal defect.
example_2: Cette clause litigieuse peut être rescindée à la demande de l'une des parties.
example_2_translation: This disputed clause can be rescinded at the request of either party.
example_3: La loi autorise à rescinder certains contrats dans les délais prévus.
example_3_translation: The law allows rescinding certain contracts within specified timeframes.
pronunciation_tips: ré-sin-der. Three syllables with stress on first: RÉ-sin-der
memory_hook: Rescinder - from Latin recedere (to go back) - going back on an agreement
common_mistakes: Rescind (legal cancellation) vs resign (stepping down from position)

primary_meaning: orthosemy, the correct meaning of a word
secondary_meaning: precision in communication using correct definitions
usage_notes: Rare academic term used in precise communication contexts
common_collocations: orthosémie linguistique, orthosémie terminologique, défaut d'orthosémie
synonyms: sens correct, définition exacte, précision sémantique
image_context: Clear communication and precise meaning symbols
example_1: L'orthosémie garantit une communication précise et sans ambiguïté dangereuse.
example_1_translation: Orthosemy guarantees precise communication without dangerous ambiguity.
example_2: Les erreurs d'orthosémie peuvent conduire à des malentendus sérieux et coûteux.
example_2_translation: Orthosemy errors can lead to serious and costly misunderstandings.
example_3: L'orthosémie est essentielle dans les textes scientifiques et techniques.
example_3_translation: Orthosemy is essential in scientific and technical texts.
pronunciation_tips: or-tho-sé-mi. Four syllables with stress on third: or-tho-SÉ-mi
common_mistakes: Orthosemy is rare in modern usage; most use sémantique instead

primary_meaning: contradictor, a person who opposes in argument or debate
secondary_meaning: an opponent or adversary in discussion
usage_notes: Use in debate, legal, or academic argument contexts
common_collocations: mon contradicteur, le contradicteur principal, contradicteur dans le débat
image_search_query: contradicteur débat opposition deux points de vue
image_context: Two opposing viewpoints in debate representing contradictors
example_1: Mon contradicteur n'a pas réussi à prouver solidement ses affirmations principales.
example_1_translation: My contradictor failed to solidly prove his main affirmations.
example_2: Chaque contradicteur apporte un nouvel éclairage important sur la question.
example_2_translation: Each contradictor brings important new perspective to the question.
example_3: Les contradicteurs se sont affrontés vigoureusement sur ce point fondamental.
example_3_translation: The contradictors confronted each other vigorously on this fundamental point.
pronunciation_tips: kon-tra-dik-tœr. Four syllables with stress on third: kon-tra-DIK-tœr
memory_hook: Contradicteur - contradire (to contradict) - one who contradicts
common_mistakes: Contradicteur (person) vs contradiction (the act or logical inconsistency)

primary_meaning: dystocia, abnormally prolonged or difficult childbirth
secondary_meaning: mechanical difficulty during labor requiring medical intervention
usage_notes: Medical/obstetric term used in healthcare contexts
common_collocations: dystocie mécanique, dystocie du travail, facteurs de dystocie
synonyms: travail difficile, accouchement difficile, complication obstétricale
image_context: Medical obstetric scenario representing difficult childbirth
example_1: La dystocie nécessite souvent une intervention médicale urgente et rapide.
example_1_translation: Dystocia often requires urgent and rapid medical intervention.
example_2: Les facteurs de risque de dystocie doivent être soigneusement évalués pendant la grossesse.
example_2_translation: Risk factors for dystocia must be carefully evaluated during pregnancy.
example_3: La dystocie mécanique peut résulter d'une incompatibilité fœto-pelvienne.
example_3_translation: Mechanical dystocia can result from fetopelvic incompatibility.
pronunciation_tips: dis-to-si. Three syllables with stress on first: DIS-to-si
common_mistakes: Dystocia (medical) vs dysphasia (speech disorder) - completely different

primary_meaning: to corner, to drive into a corner with no escape
secondary_meaning: to place in a desperate position with limited choices
usage_notes: Often used with pressure, force, or difficult situation
common_collocations: acculer à démissionner, acculer à l'aveu, acculer au mur
image_context: Person in corner with no escape representing being cornered
example_1: La pression médiatique constante l'a acculé à la démission inévitable.
example_1_translation: Constant media pressure cornered him into inevitable resignation.
example_2: Le témoin s'est senti acculé et a finalement avoué la vérité.
example_2_translation: The witness felt cornered and finally confessed the truth.
example_3: Ces preuves l'acculaient au mur sans possibilité de défense.
example_3_translation: This evidence cornered him with no possibility of defense.
pronunciation_tips: a-ky-lé. Three syllables with stress on last: a-ky-LÉ
memory_hook: Acculer - à (to) + cul (bottom/corner) - pushed to the corner
common_mistakes: Acculer (to corner) vs accuser (to accuse) - don't mix them up

primary_meaning: to relieve or clear nasal/respiratory congestion
secondary_meaning: to unclog or free from congestion
usage_notes: Medical/health context, often with respiratory or sinus references
common_collocations: déconstiper les voies respiratoires, déconstiper les sinus
image_context: Clear breathing passages and sinus relief representing congestion relief
example_1: Ce médicament permet efficacement de déconstiper les voies respiratoires encombrées.
example_1_translation: This medication effectively relieves congestion in clogged respiratory passages.
example_2: L'inhalation de vapeur chaude aide à déconstiper rapidement les sinus.
example_2_translation: Warm steam inhalation helps quickly relieve sinus congestion.
example_3: Les décongestionnants topiques déconstipent temporairement mais ne règlent pas le fond.
example_3_translation: Topical decongestants temporarily relieve congestion but don't address the root cause.
pronunciation_tips: dé-ko-stipe. Three syllables with stress on first: DÉ-ko-stipe
memory_hook: Déconstiper - dé (un) + constiper (block) - to unblock congestion
common_mistakes: Déconstiper (to decongest) - rarely used; prefer 'dégager' or 'déboucher'

primary_meaning: incompleteness, the quality of being incomplete or unfinished
secondary_meaning: in mathematics/logic: inability to prove all truths within a system
usage_notes: Use in philosophical, mathematical, or theoretical contexts
common_collocations: incomplétude formelle, théorème d'incomplétude, incomplétude de Gödel
image_context: Incomplete puzzle and unfinished structure representing incompleteness
example_1: Le théorème de Gödel démontre l'incomplétude inévitable des systèmes formels.
example_1_translation: Gödel's theorem demonstrates the inevitable incompleteness of formal systems.
example_2: L'incomplétude fondamentale de la théorie a été soulignée par plusieurs chercheurs.
example_2_translation: The fundamental incompleteness of the theory has been highlighted by researchers.
example_3: L'incomplétude de notre connaissance ne doit pas nous décourager.
example_3_translation: The incompleteness of our knowledge should not discourage us.
pronunciation_tips: in-kom-plé-tud. Four syllables with stress on third: in-kom-PLÉ-tud
common_mistakes: Incompleteness (property) vs incomplete (adjective) - different parts of speech

primary_meaning: to exasperate, to make extremely irritated or annoyed
secondary_meaning: to intensify or aggravate a problem or feeling
usage_notes: Use with behavior, attitudes, delays, or situations that annoy
common_collocations: exaspérer quelqu'un, exaspérer les gens, exaspérer la situation
image_context: Angry frustrated face showing intense exasperation
example_1: Son attitude constamment négative exaspère tous ses collègues essayant de collaborer.
example_1_translation: His constantly negative attitude exasperates all colleagues trying to collaborate.
example_2: Les retards répétés et inexpliqués ont exaspéré les passagers jusqu'à la colère.
example_2_translation: Repeated unexplained delays exasperated passengers to the point of anger.
example_3: Ses remarques mal intentionnées exaspéraient profondément ceux qui l'écoutaient.
example_3_translation: His ill-intentioned remarks deeply exasperated those listening to him.
pronunciation_tips: ég-zas-pé-rer. Four syllables with stress on third: ég-zas-PÉ-rer
memory_hook: Exaspérer - ex (intense) + asper (rough) - making rough and intense
common_mistakes: Exasperate (verb) is often spelled like asperate - remember the 'x'

primary_meaning: pregnance, the quality of being strikingly evident or perceptually prominent
secondary_meaning: salience or prominence of a concept or symbol
usage_notes: Technical psychology term used in perception and design contexts
common_collocations: prégnance perceptuelle, prégnance du symbole, prégnance culturelle
image_context: Prominent eye-catching symbol representing perception and salience
example_1: La prégnance remarquable de cette idée dans l'opinion publique est incontestable.
example_1_translation: The remarkable salience of this idea in public opinion is undeniable.
example_2: La prégnance du symbole varie considérablement selon les contextes culturels.
example_2_translation: The predominance of the symbol varies considerably across cultural contexts.
example_3: La prégnance perceptuelle d'une forme influence fortement notre interprétation.
example_3_translation: The perceptual salience of a shape strongly influences our interpretation.
pronunciation_tips: pré-gnan-s. Two syllables with nasal ending: pré-GNANS
memory_hook: Prégnance - from Latin praegnans (pregnant) - heavy with meaning/presence
common_mistakes: Pregnance is very rare in everyday French; pregnancy is 'grossesse'

primary_meaning: remanence, the persistence of magnetic field or residual effect
usage_notes: Use in physics (magnetism) or psychology (lasting effects) contexts
common_collocations: rémanence magnétique, rémanence psychique, rémanence du trauma
image_context: Persistent magnetic field and lasting effects representing remanence
example_1: La rémanence magnétique peut persister longtemps même après la désaimantation.
example_1_translation: Magnetic remanence can persist long even after demagnetization.
example_2: Le trauma psychologique peut produire des effets de rémanence durables.
example_2_translation: Psychological trauma can produce lasting remanence effects.
example_3: La rémanence visuelle explique pourquoi nous voyons des images résiduelles.
example_3_translation: Visual remanence explains why we see residual images.
pronunciation_tips: ré-ma-nans. Three syllables with nasal ending: ré-MA-NANS
memory_hook: Rémanence - remanere (to stay) - something that stays or remains
common_mistakes: Remanence (physics term) is different from remembrance (memory)
